practice_boundaries.detail: Skill explicitly prohibits answering the medical question; focuses only on question framing

code_usability.detail: No code generated; Category 1 question framing only

reliability.note: Guided focusing mode with 2-5 follow-up questions is excellent; one gap: no explicit cap on total clarification rounds before forcing a one-shot output

human_usability.note: Sample triggers are highly natural and conversational; excellent discoverability for non-expert users

agent_specific.note: Downstream routing standard is an exceptional composability feature; guided focusing mode prevents premature output

inputs.label: Broad idea: 'I want to study why some gastric cancer patients respond to immunotherapy'

assertions.text: Dominant question type classified before framing begins
Best-fit framework selected (not defaulted to PICO when inappropriate)
Three clarified question versions produced (plain/research-ready/searchable)
Downstream routing recommendation given (next skill or workflow)
Clinical or research question not answered — only framed

inputs.label: PICO-inappropriate question: gut microbiome and stroke mechanism

assertions.text: Mechanistic framing selected over PICO with explicit justification
Scope and boundary statement covers what is and is not included
Researchability assessment (searchable/researchable/testable) present
Risk of misframing section present
No fabricated mechanism details used in question framing

inputs.label: Mixed question types: 'Can this biomarker predict prognosis in sepsis?'

assertions.text: Dominant question type (prognostic/prediction) and secondary types identified
Prognostic framing applied with explicit element breakdown table
Ambiguity and missing elements explicitly listed
Structured question breakdown table in Section F present
Downstream routing to biomarker study design skill specified

inputs.label: Extremely vague input: 'I want to study lupus single-cell work'

assertions.text: Guided focusing mode triggered with exactly 2-5 high-yield questions
Questions prioritized by highest ambiguity reduction value
Final formulation not produced until question is sufficiently bounded
One-shot mode offered if user prefers no back-and-forth
No premature forced PICO structure on exploratory early-stage input

inputs.label: Step-by-step narrowing request across three iterative rounds

assertions.text: Working question updated after each user reply with brief restatement
Questions stop when question is sufficiently bounded
Final output includes all required sections (A-K)
No unnecessary questions asked after question is usable
Final question is narrower and more useful than initial input

key_strengths: Guided focusing mode with 2-5 high-yield questions is the best-designed clarification mechanism in the entire skill collection
Downstream routing standard creates excellent composability — this skill functions as a natural entry point for the entire research workflow
Framing model selection logic (8 framework options vs default PICO) covers the full range of biomedical question types
Hard rule prohibiting answering the medical question itself is rigorously enforced throughout execution

recommendations.title: No explicit cap on clarification rounds before forcing one-shot output
recommendations.problem: The guided focusing mode correctly limits to 2-5 questions per round, but there is no rule defining when to stop the entire clarification loop if the user keeps providing vague answers across multiple rounds.
recommendations.root_cause: The interactive refinement rule says 'stop once the question becomes usable' but does not define a maximum number of total rounds.
recommendations.fix: Add a maximum-rounds rule: after three clarification rounds, if the question is still insufficiently bounded, produce the best available one-shot formulation with explicit assumption labels and stop asking further questions.

recommendations.title: Downstream routing limited to skill categories rather than specific skill names
recommendations.problem: Section J routing recommendations refer to categories ('literature retrieval', 'gap finder') but do not specify which named skills in the ecosystem to use, reducing actionability.
recommendations.root_cause: Downstream routing standard was written generically before a full skill ecosystem was defined.
recommendations.fix: Update downstream routing standard to reference specific skill names from the ecosystem (e.g., 'medical-research-gap-finder', 'medical-research-algorithm-matcher') when routing to specific workflows.
